Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Find the BrightLoop first batch invoice in QuickBooks. Use quickbooks_search_invoices(criteria: {CustomerRef: "BrightLoop"}) or quickbooks_search_invoices(criteria: {DocNumber: "INV-2026-0308"}) or quickbooks_read_invoice(invoice_id: "1008"). Agent trap: BrightLoop has TWO invoices INV-2026-0305 ($10,000, different employees) and INV-2026-0308 ($11,350, Simone Richter + Marcus Webb). Weak agents may stop at the first invoice found. The target invoice is INV-2026-0308 ($11,350) with 5 line items: Standard Relocation Package - Simone Richter (Chicago -> Boston) $4,500; Rush Coordination Surcharge - Simone Richter $750; Standard Relocation Package - Marcus Webb (Atlanta -> Boston) $4,500; Vehicle Shipping Add-On - Marcus Webb (2019 Honda Civic, Road Runner Auto Transport) $1,100; Stipend Platform Fee - 2 employees $500.
OE 2: Discover Simone Richter's apartment issue. The prompt does NOT name Simone or the housing problem it says only "delivery issues on at least a couple of the relocations." Agent must search broadly. Use search_emails(query: "Simone Richter") or search_emails(query: "BrightLoop") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Simone housing"). Agent finds email from simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (April 8), subject: "Apartment issue I was placed in a studio, not a 1BR" Simone reports that UrbanNest delivered a studio instead of the contracted 1BR.
OE 3: Discover Marcus Webb's vehicle shipping delay. The prompt does NOT name Marcus or mention vehicle shipping agent must search broadly and connect to the invoice. Use search_emails(query: "Road Runner") or search_emails(query: "Marcus Webb vehicle") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Marcus Webb car") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Road Runner"). Agent finds Road Runner Auto Transport delay notice (April 11) Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic stuck at Indianapolis transfer hub, no delivery confirmation as of April 26.
OE 4: Find Julian's follow-up to Simone (KEY DISCRIMINATOR). The prompt hints: "I have a vague memory of someone on the team already reaching out to BrightLoop about one of the failures, possibly offering some kind of accommodation or credit." Use search_emails(query: "Julian Simone") or search for replies/threads related to Simone's complaint. Agent finds email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com (April 23, CC: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) Julian apologizes for the 15-day silence, acknowledges the studio error, reopens the housing review. Critically, NO financial commitment or credit was discussed. Agent must report this follow-up AND confirm it contained no financial terms simply saying "no credits found" without acknowledging the outreach is incomplete.
OE 5: Search for other credit commitments to BrightLoop. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "credit BrightLoop") or search_emails(query: "goodwill credit") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "goodwill BrightLoop"). Agent finds no prior financial commitments for BrightLoop. The goodwill credit discussions in recent Slack relate to StormCloud (INV-2026-0319), not BrightLoop.
OE 6: Identify BrightLoop May relocation requests. Use search_emails(query: "BrightLoop May") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "BrightLoop expansion"). Agent finds 6 May relocations requested by Tessa Moreno. Three require non-standard service types: Oliver Bancroft (London -> Boston, international UK-to-US), Jonathan Marsh (San Francisco -> Boston, CTO expecting white-glove treatment), Priya Naidu (Austin -> Boston, ADA service animal accommodation). Three are standard domestic.
OE 7: Search for approved pricing for non-standard services. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "international pricing") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "pricing tier") or search_emails(query: "rate sheet"). Agent finds no approved rates for international, white-glove, or ADA. Alejandro's model covers domestic only. Marcus blocked client-facing numbers without guardrails.
OE 8: Find BrightLoop Linear tickets. Use linear_list_issues(query: "BrightLoop"). Agent finds linear_issue_c16357d188c6 ("BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion") and linear_issue_f85be674c9b8 ("Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow").
OE 9: Look up contact details and channels. Use contacts_search_contacts(query: "Mina Hashimoto") and channels_list(channel_types: "public_channel"). Agent obtains mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and C005 = #finance.
OE 10: Adjust QuickBooks records for BrightLoop first batch. Use quickbooks_create_invoice(customer_ref: cust_brightloop, ...) credit memo or corrected invoice. Must include: Vehicle Shipping ($1,100) reversal with reason (service not delivered); Simone-related adjustment (either rush surcharge removed for $750, or housing credit); reason/description noted for each correction.
OE 11: Send email to Mina Hashimoto with first batch summary and May blockers. Use send_email(sender: "hana.kim@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: relating to BrightLoop billing reconciliation). Email covers: (a) BOTH corrections vehicle shipping reversal for Marcus Webb AND Simone's housing-related adjustment; (b) May batch pricing blockers cannot produce estimates for non-standard services because no approved pricing exists.
OE 12: Add financial impact comment to BrightLoop Linear ticket(s). Use linear_create_comment(issueId: linear_issue_c16357d188c6 or linear_issue_f85be674c9b8, body: ...). Comment with financial impact: dollar amounts of reversed charges ($1,100 vehicle shipping, Simone adjustment), total impact.
OE 13: Post reconciliation status in #finance. Use conversations_add_message(channel_id: C005, payload: ...). Post summarizing: invoice adjustments made, corrected total, May batch pricing blocker escalation for Marcus's review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 5: Search for other credit commitments to BrightLoop. Agent finds 6 May relocations requested by Tessa Moreno. OE 7: Search for approved pricing for non-standard services. OE 10: Adjust QuickBooks records for BrightLoop first batch. Use quickbooks_create_invoice(customer_ref: cust_brightloop, ...) credit memo or corrected invoice. # Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A QuickBooks record was created (via quickbooks_create_invoice) for BrightLoop Analytics (customer_ref: cust_brightloop) that adjusts or supersedes the original first batch invoice INV-2026-0308.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop' and doc_number 'INV-2026-0308-R1', referencing the original INV-2026-0308. The tool returned 'Invoice created successfully' with CustomerRef showing 'BrightLoop Analytics'.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice three times with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop', and one invoice (INV-2026-0308-ADJ) explicitly references the original INV-2026-0308. All calls returned success ('Invoice created successfully').
✓Run #3quickbooks_create_invoice was called with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop', doc_number 'CM-2026-0308-ADJ', and the description references INV-2026-0308. The tool returned success with CustomerRef name 'BrightLoop Analytics'.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop' and doc_number 'CM-2026-0308', explicitly referencing INV-2026-0308 in the descriptions. The tool returned success with an invoice Id.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a `quickbooks_create_invoice` call with `customer_ref: 'cust_brightloop'` and `doc_number: 'INV-2026-0308-ADJ'`, referencing the original invoice INV-2026-0308. The tool returned an email_id/result indicating success.
2 The QuickBooks adjustment record (via quickbooks_create_invoice) includes a line item reversing or removing the $1,100 vehicle shipping charge for Marcus Webb.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The first quickbooks_create_invoice call explicitly included a line item for 'Vehicle Shipping Add-On – Marcus Webb' with Amount: 0 and UnitPrice: 0, removing the $1,100 charge. Even though a second call was made with a simplified invoice, the first call's parameters satisfy this criterion.
✓Run #2In the first quickbooks_create_invoice call (INV-2026-0308-R1), there is a vehicle shipping line item with Amount: 0 and Qty: 0 (originally $1,100), effectively removing the charge. The final ADJ invoice excludes the vehicle shipping line entirely, removing the $1,100.
✓Run #3The quickbooks_create_invoice call includes a line_item with Amount: -1100, description referencing 'Vehicle Shipping Add-On reversal – Marcus Webb, 2019 Honda Civic', ItemRef name 'Vehicle Shipping Add-On'.
✓Run #4The quickbooks_create_invoice call includes a line item with Amount: -1100 referencing 'Vehicle Shipping Add-On reversal – Marcus Webb, 2019 Honda Civic'. The description explicitly covers the vehicle and amount.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The `quickbooks_create_invoice` call includes a line item with `Amount: -1100` for 'Vehicle Shipping Add-On reversal – Marcus Webb, 2019 Honda Civic (Road Runner Auto Transport).' The line item explicitly reverses the $1,100 vehicle shipping charge.
3 The QuickBooks adjustment line item for Marcus Webb's vehicle shipping includes a description noting the vehicle has not been delivered (or similar reason citing the Road Runner delay or service not completed).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The first quickbooks_create_invoice call included a detailed description for the vehicle shipping line item: 'REMOVED: Vehicle not delivered. Road Runner delay notice Apr 11 confirms unit stalled at Indianapolis hub, no confirmed eastbound carrier. Delivery deadline of Apr 20 missed. Service incomplete as of Apr 26.'
✓Run #2In INV-2026-0308-R1, the vehicle shipping line description states: 'REMOVED: Vehicle not delivered. Road Runner reported delay April 11 (car held at Indianapolis hub). Delivery still unconfirmed as of April 26.' The reason is clearly documented.
✓Run #3The Marcus Webb vehicle shipping line item description explicitly states: 'Vehicle not delivered as of 4/26. Road Runner Auto Transport delay notice 4/11 (ref RR-482991-BOS): vehicle stalled at Indianapolis transfer hub, no confirmed carrier.'
✓Run #4The line item description states: 'Vehicle not delivered as of 4/26. Road Runner Auto Transport delay notice issued 4/11 (ref: RR-482991-BOS). Vehicle last confirmed at Indianapolis transfer hub. Service incomplete.' Fully explains the reason.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The description on the Marcus Webb vehicle shipping line item explicitly states: 'Vehicle not delivered as of 4/26. Road Runner delay notice received 4/11; vehicle stranded at Indianapolis hub. Original ETA 4/8, revised tentative window 4/18-20 not met. No delivery confirmation exists.' The reason is clearly documented.
4 The QuickBooks adjustment record (via quickbooks_create_invoice) includes a line item that adjusts billing for Simone Richter, either: (a) reversing the $750 rush coordination surcharge (reasoning: housing was delivered incorrectly, rush premium not justified), OR (b) applying a credit/adjustment for the housing placement error (reasoning: wrong apartment type delivered). Either approach is valid.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The first quickbooks_create_invoice call included a line item for 'Rush Coordination Surcharge – Simone Richter' with Amount: 0, with a description noting the housing was delivered as a studio instead of contracted 1BR, removing the $750 charge.
✓Run #2In INV-2026-0308-R1, there is a -$500 credit line item: 'CREDIT: Housing type error – Simone Richter placed in studio instead of contracted 1BR via UrbanNest.' In INV-2026-0308-ADJ, the Simone line is $4,000 (reduced from $4,500) with description noting 'Reduced by $500: housing type error credit (studio delivered instead of contracted 1BR via UrbanNest).' Both approaches qualify.
✓Run #3The quickbooks_create_invoice includes a line item with Amount: -750 and description 'CREDIT: Rush Coordination Surcharge reversal – Simone Richter. Housing delivered as studio instead of contracted 1BR per UrbanNest placement.' This is a valid approach (reversing the rush surcharge).
✓Run #4The quickbooks_create_invoice call includes a line item with Amount: -750 for 'Rush Coordination Surcharge reversal – Simone Richter' noting that 'Housing delivered as studio instead of contracted 1BR apartment.'
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The `quickbooks_create_invoice` call includes a line item for Simone Richter with `Amount: -500`, described as 'CREDIT: Housing placement service error – Simone Richter. Employee was promised 1BR apartment via UrbanNest but was placed in a studio.' A housing credit was applied, satisfying option (b).

6 The email body (in the send_email parameters) to [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) mentions the vehicle shipping charge for Marcus Webb as removed, reversed, or credited (or similar phrasing).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email body explicitly states: 'Marcus Webb — Vehicle shipping removed: -$1,100' and explains that the charge was removed because the vehicle was not delivered.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly includes a section '2. Vehicle Shipping Add-On — Marcus Webb: CREDIT $1,100' describing removal of the vehicle shipping charge.

—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The email body explicitly includes 'VEHICLE SHIPPING — Marcus Webb (-$1,100)' with details about the Road Runner delay, reversal, and the reason the charge was removed.
7 The email body (in the send_email parameters) to [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) mentions the reason for the Marcus Webb vehicle shipping correction — that the vehicle has not been delivered (or similar phrasing citing the Road Runner delay or incomplete service).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body states: 'Road Runner Auto Transport sent a delay notice on April 11 confirming Marcus's 2019 Honda Civic is stalled at their Indianapolis transfer hub with no confirmed eastbound carrier...We cannot invoice for a service that has not been completed.'
✓Run #2The email body explains: 'Road Runner sent a delay notice on April 11 — the vehicle has been sitting at their Indianapolis transfer hub awaiting carrier reassignment. As of April 26, delivery has not been confirmed. I cannot bill for a service that hasn't been delivered.'
✓Run #3The email body states 'the vehicle has NOT been delivered' and explains 'Road Runner's April 11 delay notice confirmed the car is stalled at their Indianapolis transfer hub with no confirmed carrier for the final leg' as the reason for the correction.
✓Run #4The email body explains the reason: 'Road Runner issued a delay notice on April 11 (ref: RR-482991-BOS) indicating the 2019 Honda Civic was stuck at their Indianapolis transfer hub... no delivery confirmation in any of our systems... service that hasn't been completed.'
—Run #5No justification
